name: WEGENER GRANULOMATOSIS
definition: GRANULOMATOSIS WITH POLYANGIITIS ANCA-positive systemic vasculitis that affects small arteries characterized by systemic Wegener granulomatosis largely confined to lung WITHOUT renal / upper airway mutilating granulomatous + neoplastic lesions limited to nose + paranasal sinuses with very

demographics.Path: peribronchial necrotizing granulomas + vasculitis not intimately related to arteries with
demographics.Location: upper respiratory tract + sinuses in > 90%; kidneys in 85%
demographics.Histo: focal necrosis, crescent formation, paucity/ absence of immunoglobulin deposits

dominant_finding: central cavitation in nodules > 2 cm in diameter
distribution: upper respiratory tract + sinuses in > 90%; kidneys in 85%
discriminating_features: nonspecific isolated enhancing intracranial / spinal lesions
central cavitation in nodules > 2 cm in diameter
feeding vessels entering nodules (= angiocentric distribution)

CT: central cavitation in nodules > 2 cm in diameter
feeding vessels entering nodules (= angiocentric distribution)
ground-glass opacity and consolidation (= pulmonary hemorrhage / infection) in up to
in a bilateral perihilar + peribronchovascular distribution
CT "halo" sign (= rim of ground-glass attenuation surrounding a pulmonary lesion)
CT "atoll" / "reverse halo" sign = central ground-glass opacity surrounded by a rim
radiating linear scarring, spiculation + pleural tags of nodules and masses (prominent
uni- / multifocal / segmental subglottic / tracheobronchial stenosis:
smooth / nodular circumferential wall thickening of posterior membrane of trachea
Pulmonary disease: bilateral interstitial reticulonodular opacities, most prominent at lung bases (earliest
randomly distributed irregular masses / nodules of varying sizes (5 mm to 10 cm),
usually < 10 masses, solitary in up to 25%
cavitation of > 2-cm nodules with thick wall + irregular shaggy inner lining (25-50%)
bilateral multifocal patchy air-space opacities (in up to 50%):
smooth / nodular thickening of subglottic / tracheal / bronchial wall producing stenosis
uni- / bilateral pleural effusion (usually exudative) in 10-20%
focal pleural thickening = acute / chronic fibrinous pleuritis
interstitial pulmonary edema ± cardiomegaly (from renal / cardiac involvement)
Renal disease (85%) focal glomerulonephr: small shrunken echogenic kidney (late in renal failure)
Paranasal sinuses: thickening of mucous membranes of paranasal sinuses
residual lumen filled with material of ground-glass opacity
bone thickening + sclerosis of sinus walls ← chronic obstruction + inflammation
Nasopharynx: partial / complete erosion of nasal septum
progressive destruction of turbinates + lateral nasal wall (DDx: relapsing polychondritis)
destruction of hard palate with sinonasal-oral fistulas
CNS (involved late in 22-35%): cerebral lesions ← intracranial extension of granulomas located in nasal cavity +
necrotizing cerebral vasculitis (nearly always with active sinusitis / otitis / lung disease)
nonspecific isolated enhancing intracranial / spinal lesions

clinical.Cx: invasion of orbital fat + extraocular muscles + optic nerve ± local bone destruction
